data.title: Worktree isolation silently disabled when read-config shim fails to resolve script path
data.description:
## Bug

When orchestrators check `worktree.isolation_enabled` via the dso shim (`.claude/scripts/dso read-config worktree.isolation_enabled`), the shim fails silently with exit 127 ('command not found: read-config') if the plugin script isn't registered in the shim's command table.

In this session, the shim error caused the variable to be assigned an empty string, which the worktree-dispatch.md Step 2 logic interprets as 'isolation disabled'. As a result, 8 sub-agents that should have run with `isolation: "worktree"` (per config value `true`) ran in shared-directory mode instead.

## Rationalization (why it wasn't caught)

I used a shell pattern `ISOLATION_ENABLED=$(.claude/scripts/dso read-config worktree.isolation_enabled 2>/dev/null || true)` — the `2>/dev/null` suppressed the error message, and the `|| true` suppressed the non-zero exit code. The result was an empty string with no visible indication that the command had failed. I then checked the value, got empty, and treated that as 'isolation disabled'. I should have validated that the command succeeded before trusting the output.

## Root Cause

The `.claude/scripts/dso` shim does not include `read-config` (only `read-config.sh`) in its command dispatch table — OR the shim correctly dispatches but the underlying script path resolves to the main repo root instead of the worktree's plugin root when called from a worktree session.

## Correct Behavior

When `worktree.isolation_enabled=true` is in `.claude/dso-config.conf`, all orchestrator sub-agent dispatches from within a worktree MUST pass `isolation: "worktree"`. The config check must fail loudly (not silently) when the read-config mechanism fails, so the fallback is detectable.

## Impact (this session)

8 sub-agent tasks ran in shared working directory mode. All produced correct outputs (different files, no commit from sub-agents), so no data was lost. But the isolation guarantee was not honored.
